rulings.Case Title: SHARON ALLEN VS. RELATED MANAGEMENT CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Sharon Allen In The Amount Of $5,205.00 Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 128.7; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 13, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, L.P. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS RELATIVE MANAGEMENT CO.), LA SALLE PRESERVATION, L.P. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF SHARON ALLEN IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,205.00 PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 128.7; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF is DENIED. Defendant has not shown that Plaintiff's filings are in violation of Section 128.7. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAVID EDWARD RUSSELL VS. PAUL RAYMOND RUSSELL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 13, 2025 line 12. DEFENDANT PAUL RUSSELL DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part with leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order, and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the allegation regarding the exemption from the Tenant Protection Act of 2019 based on the property being subject to a local just cause ordinance (Civil Code 1946.2(i)(1)). No facts are pled to support this alleged basis for exemption, which is instead controverted by the allegation in the notice that the tenancy is not subject to the local just cause ordinance (SF Admin. Code 37.9). The demurrer is otherwise overruled. The allegations in the notice are incorporated into the complaint and must be taken as true for the purpose of demurrer. The additional or different facts argued by both parties that are not apparent on the face of the complaint were not considered. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice of a demurrer in a previous unrelated action based on materially different factual allegations is denied. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
